market_summary: REGIME: Mixed/Risk-On lean. SPY +0.06% 1m, +5.49% 3m, above both MAs (vol20=12.5%, dd63=-2.6%) — broad market stable but momentum narrowing. QQQ -1.40% 1m signals tech rotation pressure; IWM +0.23% 1m and +12.68% 3m confirms small/mid cap rotation and risk-on breadth beneath the surface. TLT +2.15% 1m suggests mild rates rally (flight-to-quality bid coexisting with equity strength) — mixed signal, not full risk-off.

LEADERSHIP: Commodities dominate momentum rankings: GLD mom=+38.77% (3m=+26.5%, 18% portfolio weight delivering), SLV mom=+89.63% (extreme outlier, 1m=-18.6% shows mean-reversion risk). Semis leadership intact: KLAC mom=+57.30% and TSM mom=+57.12% are the highest-quality momentum names with manageable drawdowns. Energy surge continuing: XOM +11.28% 1m / +28.16% 3m and CVX +11.06% 1m / +24.03% 3m both outperforming SPY meaningfully. Industrials (XLI +6.83% 1m, +17.76% 3m) sustaining rotation thesis. Weakness concentrated in hyperscalers: MSFT mom=-12.47%, AMZN mom=-6.19%, META mom=-1.91% — tech ex-semis in drawdown mode.

GO/NO-GO: Non-rebalance day (Day 1 post-Feb-25 run, 7-day minimum spacing not yet cleared — next eligible rebalance window is Mar 4 or later). Portfolio health is solid: zero thesis-damage flags, all holdings above emergency thresholds. Qualitative refresh (LLY/JPM/XOM/KLAC fundamentals) confirms: LLY Q4 EPS $7.40 beat consensus, Q1 2026 analyst estimate $7.35 with accelerating revenue ramp ($17.6B→$19.3B); JPM Q4 EPS $4.64 with 2026 consensus $5.30-$5.43 per quarter (steady compounding); XOM Q4 EPS $1.54 with 2026 estimates $1.60-$1.83 ramping; KLAC Q2 FY2026 EPS $8.73 with forward estimates accelerating to $9.10→$9.74→$10.62. No action warranted today.

thesis: Systematic hold discipline enforced: Day 1 following Feb-25 run, 7-day minimum spacing means next eligible rebalance window is Mar 4 at earliest. No emergency override conditions are met — all 8 holdings are above invalidation thresholds and zero thesis-damage flags are present.

Qualitative refresh validates portfolio construction: LLY Q4 2025 EPS $7.40 (beat) with FY2026 consensus $7.35/$8.21/$9.18/$9.47 per quarter showing strong acceleration and 83% gross margin / 39.5% operating margin / ROE=97.9% — quality compounder thesis intact despite -3.03% 1m momentum softness. KLAC FY2026-Q2 EPS $8.73 with forward estimates $9.10→$9.74→$10.62→$11.53 — semiconductor process control dominance confirmed, 43.3% operating margin and 95.2% ROE are elite. XOM at P/E=17.6x with 4.7% FCF yield and $1.60-$1.83 EPS estimates for 2026 offers value anchor in energy surge. JPM P/E=15.8x with $5.30-$5.43 quarterly EPS estimates for 2026 remains cheapest large-bank quality name.

Next rebalance (Mar 4) key decisions flagged for preparation: (1) AVGO 3m=-2.13% vs TSM 3m=+41.34% and mom=+57.12% — AVGO underperforming relative to semiconductor peers despite 12m=+61.3%; swap candidacy grows. (2) SLV mom=+89.63% is top universe momentum but 1m=-18.6% and vol20=154.5% and dd63=-37.15% make position-sizing risk extreme — if 1m stabilizes above zero before Mar 4, a small add (4-5%) replacing part of CVX or LLY weight is plausible. (3) LMT mom=+37.77% / 3m=+41.59% / dd63=-5.23% is the cleanest new-add candidate with defense spending tailwind — under active review for Mar 4 rebalance.
risks: MACRO: Rates shock risk (TLT +2.15% 1m suggests bond bid; if this reverses sharply on hawkish Fed data, energy and financials could reprice lower simultaneously). USD strength from tariff escalation could pressure XOM/CVX international operations and compress GLD's safe-haven bid.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: LLY -3.03% 1m and below MA50 — if GLP-1 competitive dynamics (NVO's pipeline despite its own -40.4% collapse) or pricing pressure headlines emerge before Mar 4, thesis requires reassessment at 12% weight. AVGO 3m=-2.13% with dd63=-25.26% is the weakest momentum link in the tech sleeve — a further leg down below $300 would trigger emergency review.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: SLV vol20=154.5% makes it a dangerous add without careful sizing — the 1m=-18.6% mean-reversion risk is real and a full-size add would spike portfolio vol meaningfully. Any position changes at Mar 4 rebalance should be staged given energy positions at 27% combined weight and potential for oil price gap risk on macro news.
falsifiable_checks: If AVGO closes below $300 before Mar 4 rebalance window, initiate emergency review for replacement with TSM (currently $387.73, 3m=+41.34%, mom=+57.12%, dd63=-10.46%).

If GLD falls below $445 (approximately -6% from $473.42 current) before next rebalance, reassess 18% safe-haven allocation — gold parabolic extension (mom=+38.77%) with vol20=57.05% creates asymmetric drawdown risk on a macro surprise.

If LLY closes below $960 (approximately -7% from $1028.83, breaching MA200 area) before Mar 4, reduce position from 12% to 8% and flag for replacement with LMT (mom=+37.77%, dd63=-5.23%) at next eligible window.
If SLV 1m momentum turns positive (above 0%) by Mar 4, initiate scoring for a 4% allocation funded by trimming CVX from 12% to 8%, subject to portfolio-level volatility check.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Day 1 of minimum 7-day hold cadence. No condition warrants override: zero thesis-damage flags, all emergency thresholds intact, and no candidate clears the cost-hurdle vs marginal improvement. Base case: energy/gold leadership continues into Mar 4 rebalance where AVGO/TSM swap and potential LMT add will be scored with full rigor. Bull case: XOM/CVX extend momentum as crude holds, KLAC earnings growth acceleration (est. $9.10→$11.53 over next 4Q) re-rates the stock higher, GLD safe-haven bid sustained. Bear case: macro shock (tariff escalation, Fed hawkishness) hits energy and financials simultaneously; at 27% energy + 12% financials = 39% cyclical concentration, portfolio is not fully immunized. Process discipline preserved; next action window Mar 4.

constraints_check.notes: FACTOR SCORES (non-rebalance refresh): KLAC=87/100 (mom=57.3%, EPS estimates +32% FY2027 vs current, operating margin 43.3%, ROE 95.2%, dd63=-22.4% moderate); GLD=82/100 (mom=38.77%, 3m=+26.5%, macro regime fit excellent, vol20=57% penalty); XOM=78/100 (mom=28.4%, 3m=+28.2%, P/E=17.6x, FCF yield=4.7%, earnings estimates ramping); CVX=75/100 (mom=19.0%, 3m=+24%, low vol20=20.6%, dd63=-5.3%); LLY=68/100 (quality elite: OM=39.5%, ROE=97.9%, EPS growth 12%+ TTM, but 1m=-3.03% and below MA50 creates momentum penalty); XLI=72/100 (mom=18.5%, 3m=+17.8%, vol20=14.9% lowest in portfolio, dd63=-2.7%, cleanest risk-adjusted carry); JPM=65/100 (P/E=15.8x, EPS est. $5.30-$5.43 2026, stable but 3m=+2.2% weak momentum, dd63=-11.2%); AVBO=58/100 (12m=+61.3% quality, but 3m=-2.13% and dd63=-25.3% apply penalties). RISK BUDGET: Max position GLD=18% (<=40% OK); Energy sector XOM+CVX=27% (<80% OK); Technology KLAC+AVGO=20% (<80% OK); Crypto=0% (<=10% OK); Total=100%. SCENARIO STRESS: Equity shock -15% SPY: portfolio est. -10% (energy/gold defensive buffer). Rates shock +100bps: GLD -5-8% (18% wt = -0.9 to 1.4% portfolio), XOM/CVX modest positive. Crude oil -20%: XOM/CVX -15-20% on 27% wt = -4 to -5.4% portfolio impact — primary tail risk. USD +5%: GLD -3%, XOM/CVX mixed, LLY international -1-2%; manageable. EXECUTION PLAN: Hold all 8 positions through Mar 4 rebalance window. At Mar 4: score AVGO vs TSM (primary swap candidate), LMT as new-add (replace CVX trim or JPM trim), SLV sizing if 1m momentum recovers. KILL-SWITCH TRIGGERS: AVGO<$300 (emergency review), GLD<$445 (resize from 18%), LLY<$960 (reduce to 8%), energy combined>30% on price appreciation (trim XOM to 12%).
